summary: This cluster focuses on the discovery and characterization of novel algal biodiversity in under-explored, deep-water marine ecosystems, specifically within the Hawaiian Archipelago. The papers are connected by their investigation of the order Peyssonneliales (Rhodophyta), culminating in the description of a new species, *Sonderophycus copusii*, from Kure Atoll. Key findings were derived from integrating morphological analysis—such as observations of secondary perithallial growth and rhizoid emergence—with molecular phylogenetic methods to delineate new taxa. The geographic scope is centered on the mesophotic benthic communities of the Northwestern Hawaiian Islands, highlighting the significant, previously undocumented diversity of red algae at these depths.
